column1: # Prestin (SLC26A5) — convergent molecular evolution for echolocation.
# Prestin is the motor protein of cochlear outer hair cells. Echolocating
# (laryngeal) bats and toothed whales independently evolved many of the same
# prestin substitutions for high-frequency hearing — a textbook case of
# molecular convergence. Famously, a tree built from prestin can pull the
# echolocators together AGAINST the species tree: watch whether the "_echo"
# species (a horseshoe bat + toothed whales) group with each other rather than
# with their true relatives (e.g. the non-echolocating baleen Blue_whale).
# Columns: UniProt accession <TAB> row label ("_echo" = laryngeal echolocator)
